title: Professional Relationships, Community Participation, and Consent · Career Claim Defense
summary: A hiring panel compares near-miss decisions for professional relationships, community participation, and consent and accepts only role-relevant, truthful, accessible, privacy-preserving, and independently defensible evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for reciprocal relationship practice plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Map existing reciprocal relationships across work, study, community, identity-safe spaces, events, and weak ties without ranking human worth.
Make a bounded, easy-to-decline request for information, feedback, introduction, or context with purpose and time expectations.
Contribute relevant help, documentation, testing, facilitation, learning notes, or community work within actual competence and norms.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Network Map
steps.instruction:
Order the Network Map evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Network Map.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Network Map assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Network Map, keep Network Map connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: Automated messages ask strangers for referrals, ignore community norms and stop signals, and measure every relationship by immediate hiring access. The quiz variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Map existing reciprocal relationships across work, study, community, identity-safe spaces, events, and weak ties without ranking human worth.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Network Map has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Network Map, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Network Map precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Network Map, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: Automated messages ask strangers for referrals, ignore community norms and stop signals, and measure every relationship by immediate hiring access. The quiz variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Map existing reciprocal relationships across work, study, community, identity-safe spaces, events, and weak ties without ranking human worth.

For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Network Map, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: People without industry contacts have no professional network.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints for the quiz Network Map case
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure for the quiz Network Map case
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence for the quiz Network Map case
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise for the quiz Network Map case

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Relationship Integrity
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Relationship Integrity: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Automated messages ask strangers for referrals, ignore community norms and stop signals, and measure every relationship by immediate hiring access. The quiz variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Refuse deceptive familiarity, hidden automation, testimonial fabrication, referral pressure, or contact harvesting.

Evidence target: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Relationship Integrity.
steps.why: Retrieving Relationship Integrity through the concrete reciprocal relationship practice plan case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Relationship Integrity, keep Relationship Integrity connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Refuse deceptive familiarity, hidden automation, testimonial fabrication, referral pressure, or contact harvesting.

hints: For Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Relationship Integrity, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Relationship Integrity precondition, or invariant that must hold.
